era_true.statement: The Doctor is a Time Lord from the planet Gallifrey.
The Doctor's TARDIS is stuck in the shape of a 1960s British police box.
TARDIS stands for Time And Relative Dimension In Space.
The TARDIS is bigger on the inside than the outside.
The Doctor can regenerate into a new body when near death.
Time Lords have thirteen lives in a standard regeneration cycle.
The Doctor was granted a new regeneration cycle by the Time Lords on Trenzalore.
The Daleks were created by Davros on the planet Skaro.
The Cybermen originated on the planet Mondas.
The Weeping Angels are quantum-locked creatures that turn to stone when observed.
The Master is a renegade Time Lord and the Doctor's oldest enemy.
The Doctor stole the TARDIS from Gallifrey when fleeing with Susan.
Susan Foreman was the Doctor's granddaughter and first companion.
The Sonic Screwdriver does not work on wood.
The Time War was fought between the Time Lords and the Daleks.
The Doctor used the Moment to end the Time War.
Gallifrey was frozen in a pocket universe rather than destroyed in the Time War.
Rose Tyler was trapped in a parallel universe after the Battle of Canary Wharf.
Bad Wolf was a message Rose Tyler scattered through time and space.
The Tenth Doctor regenerated after absorbing radiation to save Wilfred Mott.
River Song is the daughter of Amy Pond and Rory Williams.
River Song was conceived aboard the TARDIS while it traveled through the Time Vortex.
River Song married the Doctor at Lake Silencio in an aborted timeline.
The Silence are creatures that are forgotten the moment you look away from them.
Captain Jack Harkness became immortal after Rose Tyler resurrected him using the Time Vortex.
Captain Jack Harkness founded the Torchwood Institute in Cardiff.
The Ood are a telepathic species that carry their secondary brains in their hands.
K-9 is a robotic dog who traveled with the Fourth Doctor.
The Brigadier led UNIT and worked closely with the Third Doctor.
UNIT stands for Unified Intelligence Taskforce.
The Third Doctor was exiled to Earth by the Time Lords.
The Fourth Doctor offered enemies jelly babies.
The Eleventh Doctor wore a bow tie and a fez.
The Tenth Doctor wore Converse trainers with his suit.
Donna Noble had her memory of the Doctor erased to save her life.
The Doctor-Donna was a human-Time Lord metacrisis involving Donna Noble.
The Pandorica was a prison built specifically to contain the Doctor.
Rory Williams waited nearly two thousand years guarding the Pandorica as an Auton.
Amy Pond grew up with a crack in her bedroom wall caused by the TARDIS exploding.
The Impossible Girl Clara Oswald was scattered throughout the Doctor's timeline.
Clara Oswald entered the Doctor's timestream on Trenzalore to save him.
The Great Intelligence attempted to destroy the Doctor by entering his timestream.
The War Doctor was a previously unknown incarnation who fought in the Time War.
The Twelfth Doctor spent four and a half billion years inside the Confession Dial.
Ashildr became functionally immortal after the Doctor used a Mire repair kit on her.
The Zygons are shapeshifting aliens that can replicate the appearance of humans.
The Sontarans are a clone warrior race engaged in perpetual war with the Rutans.
The Judoon are rhinoceros-like intergalactic police for hire.
The Silurians are a reptilian species that inhabited Earth before humans.
The Ice Warriors are a Martian species from the planet Mars.
The Vashta Nerada are microscopic swarm creatures that live in shadows.
The Library is a planet-sized repository of every book ever written.
River Song died saving everyone in the Library and was uploaded to its computer.
The Face of Boe gave the Doctor the message "You Are Not Alone."
Professor Yana was the Master hidden at the end of the universe using a Chameleon Arch.
A Chameleon Arch rewrites a Time Lord's biology to make them human.
The Doctor's name is a closely guarded secret.
The Fields of Trenzalore are where the Doctor was prophesied to fall.
The Eleventh Doctor defended the town of Christmas on Trenzalore for centuries.
The crack in time was caused by the TARDIS exploding on June 26, 2010.
The Pandorica Alliance included Daleks, Cybermen, Sontarans, and other enemies of the Doctor.
The Ninth Doctor had big ears and wore a leather jacket.
The Ninth Doctor regenerated after absorbing the Time Vortex from Rose Tyler.
Martha Jones walked the Earth for a year spreading the Doctor's name.
The Year That Never Was occurred during the Master's rule over Earth.
The Master used the Archangel Network to control humanity.
Harold Saxon was an identity used by the Master.
Missy is a female incarnation of the Master.
Missy was executed on a Mondasian colony ship and regenerated into the Saxon Master's timeline.
Bill Potts was converted into a Cyberman on a Mondasian colony ship.
Bill Potts was saved by Heather, who transformed her into a sentient liquid being.
Nardole served as a companion to the Twelfth Doctor.
The Twelfth Doctor guarded the Vault containing Missy at St Luke's University.
The Cybermen from the parallel universe are called Cybus Cybermen.
Pete's World is the name given to the parallel universe where Rose Tyler became trapped.
Jackie Tyler married the parallel universe version of Pete Tyler.
The Tenth Doctor created a half-human clone of himself who stayed with Rose Tyler.
Sarah Jane Smith traveled with the Third and Fourth Doctors.
The TARDIS has a swimming pool and a library, which sometimes merge.
The Cloister Bell rings when the TARDIS is in extreme danger.
The Eye of Harmony powers the TARDIS.
Regeneration can change the Doctor's appearance, personality, and gender.
The Thirteenth Doctor was the first female incarnation of the Doctor in the main timeline.
The Thirteenth Doctor traveled with Yaz, Ryan, and Graham.
Ryan Sinclair had dyspraxia.
Graham O'Brien was a cancer survivor and Ryan's step-grandfather.
The Flux was an event that destroyed most of the universe.
The Division was a secret organization that employed the Doctor before their memories were erased.
The Timeless Child is the origin of regeneration for all Time Lords.
The Doctor was the Timeless Child, found beneath a gateway on another planet.
Tecteun adopted the Timeless Child and studied its ability to regenerate.
The Doctor has had incarnations before the First Doctor that were erased from memory.
The Fugitive Doctor was a previously unknown incarnation who worked for the Division.
The Fugitive Doctor's TARDIS was also shaped like a police box.
Gallifrey was destroyed by the Master after he discovered the Timeless Child truth.
The Cybermen created the CyberMasters using dead Time Lord bodies on Gallifrey.
The Master converted dead Time Lords into regenerating Cybermen called CyberMasters.
Rassilon was the founder of Time Lord society on Gallifrey.
Omega created the power source that made time travel possible for the Time Lords.
The Matrix on Gallifrey contains the accumulated knowledge of all Time Lords.
The High Council governs Time Lord society.
The Doctor's TARDIS has a chameleon circuit, but it is broken.
The TARDIS translation circuit allows companions to understand alien languages.
The Doctor has two hearts.
The Doctor's body temperature is lower than that of a human.
Adipose are creatures made entirely of human fat.
The Slitheen are a criminal family from the planet Raxacoricofallapatorius.
The Absorbaloff was a creature from the twin planet of Raxacoricofallapatorius called Clom.
The Empty Child was a boy named Jamie transformed by Chula nanogenes during the London Blitz.
The Tenth Doctor became human using a Chameleon Arch and lived as John Smith.
The Family of Blood hunted the Doctor while he was disguised as John Smith.
Donna Noble is the most important woman in all of creation according to prophecy.
The Fifteenth Doctor was portrayed by Ncuti Gatwa.
The Fourteenth Doctor had the same face as the Tenth Doctor.
The Toymaker is a being from beyond the universe who plays deadly games.
The Fourteenth Doctor defeated the Toymaker by winning a game of catch.
The Fourteenth Doctor underwent a bi-generation, splitting into two separate Doctors.
The Fifteenth Doctor emerged from the bi-generation as a distinct incarnation.
Ruby Sunday was a companion of the Fifteenth Doctor.
Ruby Sunday was abandoned at a church on Christmas Eve as a baby.

era_false.statement: The Doctor is originally from the planet Skaro.
The TARDIS is powered by a black hole contained in its central console.
Rose Tyler traveled with the Sixth Doctor throughout season twenty-two.
The Daleks were created by a scientist named Stavros on Mondas.
The Doctor has a maximum of ten regenerations in total.
The Cybermen originated on the planet Gallifrey.
River Song is the Doctor's biological sister.
The Master's real Gallifreyan name is Borusa.
Captain Jack Harkness became immortal after being exposed to the Time Vortex by Martha Jones.
The Weeping Angels feed on electrical energy from power grids.
The Sonic Screwdriver works perfectly on wood in every situation.
The Doctor stole the TARDIS from the Celestial Toymaker's domain.
Donna Noble remembered her time with the Doctor permanently without any consequences.
The Silence are a religious order devoted to ensuring the Doctor's survival.
K-9 was a companion originally introduced during the Fifth Doctor's era.
Rory Williams waited outside the Pandorica for two hundred years as a Sontaran.
The Time War was fought between the Time Lords and the Cybermen.
The Doctor's granddaughter Susan chose the name TARDIS.
The Tenth Doctor regenerated after absorbing radiation to save Wilfred Mott in a Dalek ship.
Amy Pond was known as the Girl Who Waited because she waited two years for the Doctor.
The TARDIS exterior is stuck as a blue police box because of a broken hyperdrive.
The Ood are a naturally aggressive warrior species from the planet Raxacoricofallapatorius.
Sarah Jane Smith first appeared as a companion to the First Doctor.
The Doctor's home planet Gallifrey is located in the Andromeda galaxy.
The Judoon are intergalactic criminals wanted across the universe.
Clara Oswald was scattered through the Doctor's timeline by the Great Intelligence.
The Twelfth Doctor chose his face because it reminded him of a Roman citizen he saved in Pompeii.
Brigadier Lethbridge-Stewart led UNIT as a Colonel throughout his entire tenure.
The Vashta Nerada are microscopic creatures that live exclusively in water.
The Doctor destroyed Gallifrey single-handedly at the end of the Time War.
Martha Jones became the Doctor's companion after winning a contest on a game show.
The Zygons are shapeshifters who require monthly doses of radiation to maintain their disguises.
The TARDIS interior has always remained the same design since the show began.
The Sontarans are a clone warrior race engaged in an eternal war against the Rutans on Earth.
Missy is a regeneration of the Rani rather than the Master.
The Ninth Doctor traveled with Rose Tyler for three full television seasons.
The Doctor has always been depicted as male throughout the show's entire history.
The Pandorica was built by the Time Lords to imprison the Master.
The Face of Boe's final message to the Doctor was "You are forgiven."
Torchwood was founded by Queen Victoria to support and assist the Doctor.
The Impossible Girl arc referred to Amy Pond's mysterious appearances throughout history.
Davros is the supreme ruler of the Cybermen and created them on Telos.
The Chameleon Circuit is the TARDIS component responsible for time travel.
The Doctor was exiled to Mars by the Time Lords during the Third Doctor's era.
The Eleventh Doctor's favorite food was fish fingers and beans.
The TARDIS stands for Time And Relative Dimensions In Space Travel.
The Slitheen are a species rather than a family from Raxacoricofallapatorius.
Bad Wolf was a message scattered through time by Captain Jack Harkness.
The Doctor's age has been consistently and precisely tracked throughout the entire series.
The War Doctor fought in the Time War between the Eighth and Tenth Doctors with no incarnation between.
Handles was the name of the Eleventh Doctor's pet Dalek.
The Testimony Foundation was created by the Daleks to harvest memories of the dead.
The Moment is a forbidden weapon that takes the form of a Dalek when activated.
The Twelfth Doctor spent four and a half thousand years inside the confession dial.
The Silurians are an alien species that colonized Earth millions of years ago.
The Doctor's daughter Jenny was created from a genetic loom on Gallifrey.
Vincent van Gogh defeated the Krafayis using his paintings as weapons.
The Impossible Astronaut who shot the Doctor at Lake Silencio was Amy Pond.
UNIT stands for United Nations Intelligence Team.
The Headless Monks are called headless because they remove their hands as a sign of faith.
The Dream Lord was a physical alien entity that infiltrated the TARDIS.
Ace was a companion of the Fourth Doctor known for her use of Nitro-9.
The Mara is a creature from the Land of Fiction that possesses people through mirrors.
The Timeless Child revelation showed that the Master was the original source of regeneration.
The Flux destroyed exactly one galaxy before the Doctor stopped it.
The Toymaker was defeated by the Fourteenth Doctor in a game of chess.
Gallifreyan children look into the Untempered Schism at the age of sixteen.
The Daleks' greatest weakness is their inability to climb stairs.
The Thirteenth Doctor traveled with Graham, Ryan, Yaz, and Mickey Smith.
The TARDIS translation circuit requires the Doctor to manually activate it for each new language.
The Tenth Doctor said "Geronimo" as his signature catchphrase.
Omega created the Eye of Harmony by detonating a supernova on Gallifrey's surface.
The Cybermen's only weakness is gold pressed against their breathing apparatus in every version.
The Great Intelligence is a benevolent entity that protects the Doctor's timeline.
The Eighth Doctor's regeneration into the War Doctor was shown in the fiftieth anniversary special.
Canton Everett Delaware III was a secret agent working for the Silence.
The Daleks exterminate by firing beams from their eyestalks.
The Doctor's cot from Gallifrey had the Doctor's birth name written on it in Old High Gallifreyan.
The Monks trilogy ended with Bill Potts defeating the Monks through physical combat.
The Fugitive Doctor was confirmed to exist between the Second and Third Doctors.
Rassilon was a humble servant of the Time Lords with no political ambitions.
The Absorbaloff was a creature that appeared in a story written by Steven Moffat.
The Carrionites use mathematics as their form of science and power.
The Doctor's TARDIS is a Type 50 time capsule.
The Pandorica Opens was the finale of series four of the revived show.
Pete's World is an alternate universe where Rose Tyler was never born.
The Tenth Doctor married Queen Elizabeth I during the events of The Shakespeare Code.
The Boneless are creatures from a two-dimensional universe that the Eleventh Doctor encountered.
Nardole is a fully human companion who joined the Doctor after meeting him on Earth.
The Stenza are a warrior race that use heat as their primary weapon.
The Twelfth Doctor guarded the Vault containing Davros beneath St Luke's University.
The Hybrid prophecy from Gallifrey predicted a creature that was half Dalek and half Cyberman.
The Ninth Doctor regenerated after absorbing Time Vortex energy from Rose Tyler.
The Family of Blood wanted the Doctor's regeneration energy to fuel their spaceship.
The Adipose are deadly parasitic creatures that kill their host immediately upon forming.
The Midnight Entity was fully explained and identified before the episode ended.
Clara entered the Doctor's timestream on Trenzalore by jumping into his grave on Gallifrey.
The Shakri cubes activated after one year of being distributed across Earth.
The Stolen Earth involved twenty-seven planets being moved to the Medusa Cascade by the Cybermen.
The Eleventh Doctor spent three hundred years defending the town of Christmas on Skaro.
Bill Potts was the first openly gay companion in the main television series and she traveled with the Eleventh Doctor.
The Huon particles inside Donna Noble were placed there by the Sontarans.
The Confession Dial is a device Time Lords use to record their happiest memories.
The Nethersphere was a virtual afterlife created by the Daleks to build a Cyber army.
The Curator who appeared in The Day of the Doctor was confirmed to be the Master in disguise.
The Tenth Doctor wiped Donna's memory because she absorbed Dalek DNA.
Ashildr became immortal after the Doctor used a Gallifreyan healing device on her.
The Siege of Trenzalore lasted approximately three weeks before the Doctor regenerated.
The Toclafane were revealed to be converted Daleks from the far future.
The Doctor fell through the floor of the TARDIS and landed in Victorian London to meet the Paternoster Gang for the first time.
The Twelfth Doctor's final words before regenerating were "I don't want to go."
The Stolen Moment was the device the War Doctor used to end the Time War by destroying Gallifrey.
The Extremis simulation was created by the Weeping Angels to study Earth's defenses.
The Timeless Child was discovered by Tecteun on the planet Gallifrey.
The Doctor has visited every planet in the universe according to the show's established canon.
The Celestial Toymaker first appeared in the revived series during the Tenth Doctor's era.
Harriet Jones was Prime Minister for three consecutive terms after the Doctor endorsed her.
The Racnoss Empress was the last survivor of a species that originated on Mars.
The Untempered Schism is a gap in the fabric of reality that shows all of space but not time.
The Thirteenth Doctor defeated the Lone Cyberman by throwing the Death Particle into the Boundary.

modern_true.statement: The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye.
Honey does not spoil when stored in sealed containers.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.
Australia is both a country and a continent.
Bananas are berries, but strawberries are not.
The human body contains more bacterial cells than human cells.
Mount Everest grows taller each year due to tectonic activity.
Venus rotates clockwise, opposite to most planets in the solar system.
The Treaty of Versailles was signed on June 28, 1919.
Finland has more saunas than cars.
A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
The speed of light in a vacuum is 299,792,458 meters per second.
Nelson Mandela was released from prison on February 11, 1990.
Iceland runs almost entirely on geothermal and hydroelectric energy.
Sharks existed before trees evolved on Earth.
The Amazon River discharges more water than any other river on Earth.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid.
DNA was first imaged using X-ray crystallography by Rosalind Franklin.
Switzerland did not grant women full suffrage until 1971.
The Pacific Ocean covers more area than all landmasses combined.
Tardigrades can survive the vacuum of outer space.
The Gutenberg printing press was invented around 1440.
Russia spans eleven time zones.
Human trafficking generates over 150 billion dollars annually worldwide.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire.
The Mariana Trench is the deepest known point in Earth's oceans.
Cows have best friends and become stressed when separated from them.
The Chernobyl nuclear disaster occurred on April 26, 1986.
Vatican City is the smallest internationally recognized country by area.
Elephants are the only animals that cannot jump.
The human genome contains roughly 20,000 protein-coding genes.
The Rosetta Stone was discovered in 1799 by French soldiers in Egypt.
South Korea has the fastest average internet speed of any nation.
Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006 by the International Astronomical Union.
The Sahara Desert was green and lush approximately 6,000 years ago.
Greenland is the largest island on Earth that is not a continent.
The transistor was invented at Bell Labs in 1947.
Bhutan measures national success using Gross National Happiness instead of GDP.
A teaspoon of neutron star material weighs about six billion tons.
The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.
Lake Baikal in Siberia contains about 20 percent of the world's unfrozen freshwater.
The human nose can detect over one trillion distinct scents.
The Rwandan genocide killed an estimated 800,000 people in 100 days in 1994.
New Zealand was the first country to grant women the right to vote in 1893.
Glass is an amorphous solid, not a liquid.
The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living structure visible from orbit.
Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun but not the hottest.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft entered interstellar space in August 2012.
Ethiopia follows a calendar that is seven years behind the Gregorian calendar.
Mitochondria contain their own separate DNA distinct from nuclear DNA.
The Dead Sea is the lowest point on Earth's surface above water.
The first successful human heart transplant was performed in 1967 by Christiaan Barnard.
There are more possible iterations of a chess game than atoms in the observable universe.
Mongolia is the most sparsely populated country in the world.
Lightning strikes the Earth about 100 times every second.
The Suez Canal opened in 1869 and connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea.
Sloths can hold their breath longer than dolphins can.
The Korean War began on June 25, 1950, and ended with an armistice in 1953.
Photons are massless particles that carry electromagnetic force.
Canada has the longest coastline of any country on Earth.
Mantis shrimp can perceive ultraviolet and polarized light invisible to humans.
The first photograph ever taken was captured by Joseph Nicéphore Niépce in 1826.
Indonesia is the world's largest archipelago nation with over 17,000 islands.
Water expands when it freezes, unlike most other substances.
The Apollo 11 Moon landing occurred on July 20, 1969.
Sanskrit is one of the oldest known languages still in liturgical use.
The Atacama Desert in Chile is the driest non-polar desert on Earth.
CRISPR gene-editing technology was adapted from a bacterial immune defense system.
The Ottoman Empire officially dissolved in 1922.
Wombat feces are cube-shaped.
The International Space Station orbits Earth roughly every 90 minutes.
Madagascar separated from the Indian subcontinent about 88 million years ago.
The printing of the King James Bible was completed in 1611.
Greenland's ice sheet holds enough water to raise global sea levels by about seven meters.
The human brain consumes about 20 percent of the body's total energy.
The Tunguska event of 1908 flattened 2,000 square kilometers of Siberian forest.
Dolphins sleep with one half of their brain at a time.
The Magna Carta was sealed in 1215 at Runnymede, England.
There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way galaxy.
Plate tectonics cause the Atlantic Ocean to widen by about 2.5 centimeters per year.
The first email was sent by Ray Tomlinson in 1971.
Bolivia has two constitutional capitals: Sucre and La Paz.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to avoid drifting apart.
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into orbit on April 24, 1990.
Arabic is written and read from right to left.
The longest war in recorded history was between the Netherlands and the Isles of Scilly, lasting 335 years.
A single bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast 100,000 slices of bread.
The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille.
Coral reefs support roughly 25 percent of all marine species.
The element helium was first detected in the Sun's spectrum before being found on Earth.
Tokyo is the most populous metropolitan area in the world.
Cats have over 20 vocalizations including the meow, purr, and chirp.
The first programmable computer, the Z3, was built by Konrad Zuse in 1941.
The Nile River flows northward through eleven countries in Africa.
Butterflies taste with their feet.
The Indian Ocean tsunami of December 26, 2004, killed over 230,000 people.
Gold is chemically inert and does not rust or tarnish under normal conditions.
The longest place name in the world belongs to a hill in New Zealand with 85 characters.
The human eye can distinguish about ten million different colors.
The Galápagos Islands are located on the equator in the Pacific Ocean.
Peanuts are legumes, not true nuts.
The first successful powered airplane flight occurred at Kitty Hawk on December 17, 1903.
South Africa has three capital cities: Pretoria, Bloemfontein, and Cape Town.
Black holes emit radiation, a phenomenon predicted by Stephen Hawking in 1974.
The Amazon rainforest produces about six percent of the world's oxygen.
The longest river in Europe is the Volga, flowing entirely within Russia.
Platypuses are venomous mammals that lay eggs and detect prey using electroreception.
The Universal Declaration of Human Rights was adopted by the United Nations in 1948.
The coldest temperature ever recorded on Earth was minus 89.2 degrees Celsius in Antarctica.
Fungi are more closely related to animals than to plants.
The Terracotta Army was discovered by farmers digging a well in Xi'an, China, in 1974.
The Sun accounts for 99.86 percent of the total mass of the solar system.
There are 195 internationally recognized sovereign nations as of 2024.
Braille was invented by Louis Braille, who was blinded in a childhood accident.
The deepest point in the ocean, Challenger Deep, lies about 10,935 meters below sea level.
The first vaccine was developed by Edward Jenner in 1796 against smallpox.
Earth's magnetic poles have reversed hundreds of times throughout geological history.
The Library of Alexandria was one of the largest and most significant libraries of the ancient world.
